label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which of the statements is true based on the given text. Let's go through each option one by one. First, the options are A, B, C, and D. The text is about computers in general and the Kurzweil machine specifically. Starting with option A: "Computers can solve all the problems people can't do." The original text says "Computers can solve difficult problems and control other machines." But it doesn't say they can solve all problems that people can't. The word "all" here might make this statement too absolute. The text mentions they can solve difficult problems, but not necessarily every possible problem people can't. So A might not be correct. Option B: "Large computers can only solve one problem at a time." The text says "Scientists use large computers to solve many problems at the same time." So this statement is the opposite of what's in the text. Large computers can handle multiple problems, so B is false. Option C: "When using the Kurzweil machine, a blind man needn't do anything." The text describes the process: "a blind person can simply open the book and put it down on the scanner. The machine 'reads' the words... When one page is finished, the blind person turns the page and puts the book on the scanner again." So the blind person has to open the book, place it on the scanner, and turn the pages. Therefore, they do have to do some things, so C is incorrect. Option D: "The Kurzweil machine can help blind people and short-sighted children."
